sources.exact_document_title: Testosterone Therapy in Men With Hypogonadism: An Endocrine Society Clinical Practice Guideline
sources.version: Journal of Clinical Endocrinology & Metabolism 2018;103(5):1715–1744; DOI 10.1210/jc.2018-00229
sources.jurisdiction: International specialty guideline; UAE laboratory, prescribing and referral policy governs local use
sources.population: Adult men with symptoms or signs compatible with testosterone deficiency and clinician-reviewed testosterone results.
sources.clinical_setting: Clinician evaluation of possible hypogonadism and monitoring after clinician-selected testosterone therapy.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for documenting symptom context, reliable morning result verification, cause evaluation, contraindication discussion and clinician follow-up. It does not diagnose hypogonadism or authorise therapy.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1–1.4 — symptoms, repeat morning result and cause evaluation
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting compatible symptoms, consistently low morning testosterone context, reliable assay or reference-range context and clinician-selected LH, FSH or further evaluation.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 2.1–2.6 — individualized treatment and risk discussion
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting fertility plans, comorbidity and contraindication context and shared benefit-risk discussion; no therapy is selected.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 3.1–3.2 — response, adverse effects and monitoring
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician review of response, adverse effects, adherence and monitoring after treatment initiation without generating thresholds, doses or referral.
